title: Public services associate professionals
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate medical and health services in hospitals, clinics, managed care organizations, public health agencies, or similar organizations. Examine, evaluate, and investigate eligibility for or conformity with laws and regulations governing contract compliance of licenses and permits, and perform other compliance and enforcement inspection and analysis activities not classified elsewhere. Inspect and investigate sources of pollution to protect the public and environment and ensure conformance with Federal, State, and local regulations and ordinances. (Based on 76 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Distribute flyers, brochures, or other informational or educational documents to inform members of a targeted community.
Advise clients or community groups on improving general health, including diet, exercise, and self-care issues like diabetes management.
Draft contract proposals, counter-proposals, rules, or regulations for collective bargaining and labor negotiations with relevant representatives.
Interpret contractual agreements and evaluate union complaints to determine viability during collective bargaining processes.
Mediating discussions between employer and employee representatives to reconcile differences, including scheduling grievance hearings.
Monitor adherence to labor agreements and compliance with central and devolved government laws, including reporting requirements.
Negotiate collective bargaining agreements and recommend strategies or objectives regarding labor relations.
Attend community meetings or health fairs to understand community issues and improve relationships with community stakeholders.
Administer immunizations, provide basic health services, and conduct home visits for monitoring high-risk individuals' health needs.
Conduct assessments to identify the health care needs of community members and report vital statistics about the general health of the community.
Advocate for individual or community health needs with government agencies or health service providers.
Prepare reports on labor relations matters, employee satisfaction, and other relevant analyses for management review.
Develop and deliver training programs for managers or supervisors related to labor relations, safety, and employee health policies.
Review and add to client records, maintaining organization and confidentiality of personnel documents.
Prepare for and participate in arbitration or negotiate collective agreements while representing the company or labor interests effectively.
Assist clients in accessing social services and understanding available health services, including childhood immunization information.
Monitor health outcomes for targeted groups, such as children, the elderly, or those with specific health risks or conditions.
Establish objectives and evaluative or operational criteria for units managed.
Evaluate recruitment or selection criteria to ensure conformance to professional standards, recommending revisions as needed.
Conduct exit interviews, ensuring necessary employment termination paperwork is completed and employment-related data is analyzed.
Confer with management to develop or implement personnel policies or procedures and advise on recruiting or retention programs.
Develop and implement organizational policies and procedures for the facility or medical unit and maintain current knowledge of regulations.
Conduct and administer fiscal operations, including budgeting and financial reporting, to ensure effective resource management.
Monitor the use of diagnostic services and assess the need for additional staff, equipment, and services in the healthcare sector.
Provide management with training on interviewing, performance evaluations, and documentation of performance issues.
Conduct worker studies to determine job-related illnesses and recommend corrective measures based on environmental analyses.
Inspect facilities and equipment to ensure compliance with safety and sanitation regulations and recommend necessary modifications.
Inform personnel about changes in regulations and policies, and explain procedures to aid their job performance.
Prepare or edit organizational publications, such as newsletters or reports, for internal or external audiences.
Confer with other managers to identify trends or key group interests or provide advice on business decisions.
Establish or maintain cooperative relationships with representatives of community, consumer, employee, or public interest groups.
Prepare reports on investigations or inspections and actions taken to ensure compliance with regulations.
Inspect shipments and cargo to determine compliance with health and safety regulations and need for maintenance.
Educate the public about health issues or enforce health legislation to promote health and prevent disease.
Collect, analyze, and report environmental data, including pollution emission measurements and atmospheric monitoring.
Monitor the environmental impact of development activities, pollution, or land degradation, and recommend methods to minimize impact.
Conduct environmental impact studies to examine the ecological effects of pollutants and human activities.
Develop and communicate recommendations for landowners to maintain or restore environmental conditions.
Work as part of a team of healthcare professionals to manage patient care by assisting with medical procedures or related duties.
Conduct various eye tests such as visual field tests, tonometry, and assess refractive conditions using specialized equipment.

Encourage patients to participate in therapeutic activities that enhance interpersonal skills and promote wellness and independence.

Train or instruct patients in utilizing adaptive equipment and assistive devices to promote independence in daily activities.

Plan, direct, and participate in recruitment and enrollment activities.

Respond to emergency situations on an on-call basis and provide emergency medical services as required.
Conduct inspections to ensure compliance with applicable fire codes, ordinances, laws, regulations, and safety standards.
Investigate violations of natural resources, waste management, and recycling policies and review or revise sustainability proposals.
Develop emergency management plans for recovery decision making, continuity of operations, and conduct contingency plan integration.
Analyze corporate intelligence data to identify security threats and investigate cases of suspected arson or hazards.

Inspect and test fire protection systems and conduct or oversee collection of corporate intelligence to avoid security threats.
Interpret government regulations to ensure compliance and recommend methods to monitor or evaluate reference standards and interruptions.
